case_title: GABRIEL ESPINOZA VS. HARBOR FREIGHT TOOLS USA, INC.

docket_entries.description: PERSONAL INJURY/PROPERTY DAMAGE - NON-VEHICLE RELATED, COMPLAINT FILED BY PLAINTIFF ESPINOZA, GABRIEL AS TO DEFENDANT HARBOR FREIGHT TOOLS USA, INC. SUMMONS ISSUED, JUDICIAL COUNCIL CIVIL CASE COVER SHEET FILED CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE SCHEDULED FOR JUN-10-2020 PROOF OF SERVICE DUE ON                  MAR-09-2020 CASE MANAGEMENT STATEMENT DUE ON         MAY-18-2020
AFFIDAVIT OF COMPLIANCE (TRANSACTION ID # 100095063) FILED BY PLAINTIFF ESPINOZA, GABRIEL
NOTICE AND ACKNOWLEDGMENT OF RECEIPT, SIGNED JAN-30-2020, SERVED JAN-14-2020 (TRANSACTION ID # 100095920) FILED BY PLAINTIFF ESPINOZA, GABRIEL AS TO DEFENDANT HARBOR FREIGHT TOOLS USA, INC.

LAW AND MOTION 302, PLAINTIFF GABRIEL ESPINOZA'S MOTION TO APPROVE PROPOSED PROPOSITION 65 SETTLEMENT AND CONSENT JUDGMENT IS GRANTED. NO OPPOSITION FILED AND THE SETTLEMENT COMPLIES WITH THE REQUIREMENTS FOR PROPOSITION 65 SETTLEMENTS UNDER HEALTH & SAFETY CODE SECTION 25249.7. THE COURT FINDS THAT THE WARNING THAT IS REQUIRED BY THE SETTLEMENT COMPLIES WITH HEALTH & SAFETY CODE SECTIONS 25249.5-25249.14; THE AWARD OF ATTORNEY'S FEES IS REASONABLE UNDER CALIFORNIA LAW; AND THE PENALTY AMOUNT IS REASONABLE BASED ON THE CRITERIA SET FORTH IN HEALTH & SAFETY CODE SECTION 25249.7(B)(2). ORDER AND CONSENT JUDGMENT ARE SIGNED. JUDGE: ETHAN P. SCHULMAN, CLERK: S. LE, COURT REPORTER: MARIA TORREANO, CSR#8600, MARIA.TORREANO@GMAIL.COM, REPORTED. =(302/EPS)

calendar.matters:
CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar May-22-2020 Off Calendar

Notice Of Motion And Motion To Approve Proposed 65 Settlement And Consent Judgment
Off Calendar Mar-18-2020 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: No appearances. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

PLAINTIFF GABRIEL ESPINOZA's Motion To Approve Proposed 65 Settlement And Consent Judgment

Plaintiff Gabriel Espinoza's motion to approve proposed Proposition 65 settlement and consent judgment is granted. No opposition filed and the settlement complies with the requirements for Proposition 65 settlements under Health & Safety Code section 25249.7. The Court finds that the warning that is required by the settlement complies with Health & Safety Code sections 25249.5-25249.14; the award of attorney's fees is reasonable under California law; and the penalty amount is reasonable based on the criteria set forth in Health & Safety Code section 25249.7(b)(2).

Order and consent judgment are signed.

Judge: Ethan P. Schulman, Clerk: S. Le, Court Reporter: Maria Torreano, CSR#8600, maria.torreano@gmail.com, Reported. =(302/EPS)
